name: grape seed
mechanism: Proposed mechanism: Proanthocyanidin-rich extracts are often discussed in relation to antioxidant and vascular effects, but human clinical outcomes depend on product composition and study design; this remains a proposed framework rather than a confirmed clinical mechanism.
activeCompounds: example reference compounds include proanthocyanidin/procyanidin B2 and catechin.
effects: No direct effects data. Contextual inference:
nan.. No direct mechanismofaction data. Contextual inference:
contraindications: Avoid during pregnancy and breastfeeding unless supervised by a qualified clinician.
Do not use with known allergy to this plant or related species.
safetyNotes: NCCIH describes grape seed extract as generally well tolerated orally or topically, with limited pregnancy/lactation safety data; LiverTox states grape seed extracts have not been associated with clinically apparent acute liver injury.

sources.title: Grape Seed Extract: Usefulness and Safety

description: Made from the seeds of wine grapes; contains proanthocyanidins described by NCCIH as having effects that might be beneficial for health-condition management.

preparation: Oral dietary supplements and topical products; NCCIH also notes limited research on mouthrinse use and topical wound-healing hypotheses.
region: POWO lists native range for *V. vinifera* as S. Central & SE Europe to Central Asia and N. Iran.
therapeuticUses: Traditional supportive use; indication depends on species part and preparation.
Evidence level varies; use only within established clinical or traditional context.
sideEffects: May cause gastrointestinal upset (nausea, cramping, or diarrhea).
Possible headache, dizziness, or allergic skin reaction in sensitive users.
summary: Grape seed extract (from wine-grape seeds) is promoted for cardiometabolic and topical uses; NCCIH cites reviews suggesting possible improvements in LDL/triglycerides and diastolic blood pressure, but emphasizes small trials and variable product composition, so conclusions are not definite.
